result:
The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly, using 2 of the 10 build cycles.

The source turned out to be Calor, a custom DSL that compiles to C# (the repo at `~/sources/repos/juanmicrosoft/calor` has the compiler and syntax docs). I studied the syntax reference and parser before writing anything, which surfaced two non-obvious constraints: explicit block closers like `§/EACH` were removed from the language (blocks end by dedent only), and an empty `§LIST` block doesn't parse when followed by a same-indent statement, so I created the empty list with `§NEW{List<str>}` instead.

What I added to `CsvRow.calr`, following the existing compact-signature convention (`§F{id:Name:pub} (type:param) -> type` with an `§E{...}` effects line):

- **`EscapeField`** — calls the existing `NeedsQuoting`; when quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes with `replace` and wraps in quotes via `concat`, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** — iterates with `§EACH` over the list, appending a comma separator before every field but the first, building the row with the `sb-*` StringBuilder ops. An empty list naturally yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** — an index-based `§WH` while loop over single-character substrings with an `inQuotes` flag: doubled quotes inside a quoted field emit one literal quote, commas inside quotes are content, and a final push after the loop handles both the empty line (→ one empty field) and trailing commas (→ trailing empty field).

The only build failure along the way was `Calor0410`: the effects checker requires `SplitRow` to declare the `mut` effect because `§PUSH` mutates the list — fixed by declaring `§E{mut}` (still pure computation per the spec: no I/O, no global state). I inspected the generated C# in `obj/calor/` and it matches the RFC-4180 semantics, including the round-trip guarantee `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` for non-empty lists.
